purpose: The Notepad++ plugin ABI shim. DllMain captures HINSTANCE and hands it to the PluginController singleton (and disables thread-attach callbacks). The five __declspec(dllexport) extern "C" functions Notepad++ looks up by name (isUnicode, setInfo, getName, getFuncsArray, beNotified, messageProc) all delegate straight to PluginController::Instance(). This is the public surface Notepad++ binds to.
calls_out_to: Notepad++ host process (these exports are resolved by GetProcAddress and called by Notepad++ during plugin load, menu population, notification dispatch, and shutdown)
invariants: isUnicode must return TRUE - this declares the plugin is Unicode and Notepad++ will hand it wide-char data.
Export names and signatures are fixed by the Notepad++ plugin ABI; renaming any of them breaks discovery.
DllMain only handles DLL_PROCESS_ATTACH; the controller is reachable through Instance() at any later time without further DllMain involvement.
setInfo may be called before getFuncsArray - the controller must be ready for either order.
gotchas: DisableThreadLibraryCalls means DLL_THREAD_ATTACH / DLL_THREAD_DETACH callbacks won't fire; any thread-local-storage that relies on them would be broken (currently none).
g_module is captured once at process-attach; setInfo also re-asserts it on the controller as a belt-and-braces measure.
